event: View of Saint Basil's Cathedral on Red Square in winter
symbols: colorful onion domes — the exuberant faith of Russian Orthodoxy
snow covering — the endurance of sacred architecture through harsh seasons
description: A painting of Saint Basil's Cathedral on Moscow's Red Square in winter. The cathedral's famous cluster of colorful onion domes rises at center — green, blue, gold, striped, and patterned — above brick and stone walls. Snow covers the ground and rooftops. A section of the Kremlin wall with a tower is visible at the right edge. A monument or statue stands in the foreground on a snow-covered circular base. The sky is overcast and pale. The painting is rendered in a loose, painterly style with visible brushwork.
